channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — Voters in Ivory Coast cast ballots Saturday to elect a new leader as longtime President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term, while his key contenders were barred from the race.
item.dc:creator: OPE ADETAYO and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Malaysian foreign minister warned his counterparts from Southeast Asia that the space for neutrality is shrinking in a region increasingly shaped by big power rivalry, as they mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump travels to Asia ahead of meeting with President Xi
item.description: “We have a lot to talk about with President Xi, and he has a lot to talk about with us," Trump told reporters as he left the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa nears hurricane strength and drops torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa has neared hurricane strength, threatening the northern Caribbean with massive rainfall, flooding, and landslides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Just days after taking office, Japan's new leader faces a series of back-to-back foreign policy tests, with a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Tokyo sandwiched between Asia-region summits in Malaysia and South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they warned of catastrophic flooding
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump to meet Chinese president next week for key trade deal: White House
item.description: President Trump is preparing for a high-stakes trip to Asia aimed at testing his reputation as a dealmaker, with a potential trade agreement with China on the line.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 48 bags of suspected human remains found at Mexican gravesite
item.description: Previous discoveries of bags found buried in Jalisco state gravesites have included bone fragments and human tissue.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Houthi rebels detain 2 more UN staffers in Yemen as the world body reevaluates its operations
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels detained two additional United Nations staff members and raided the homes of several others in Yemen over the last 48 hours, the latest in a series of events now forcing the world body to reassess how it operates in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and AHMED AL-HAJ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio tours US-led center in Israel overseeing the ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: KIRYAT GAT, Israel (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday toured a U.S.-led center in Israel overseeing the Gaza ceasefire, as the Trump administration worked to set up an international security force in the territory and shore up the tenuous truce between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: The Pentagon is sending the world’s largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford, and its carrier air wing to the Caribbean as the Trump administration conducts strikes against boats it says are smuggling illegal drugs in the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Rubio slams UNRWA as a 'subsidiary of Hamas,' vows it will not 'play any role' in delivering aid to Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio declared UNRWA won't assist Gaza aid delivery, calling the U.N. agency a "subsidiary of Hamas" amid the U.S.-brokered ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's GOP allies look to seize Trump's momentum versus Putin
item.description: Senators are hoping to seize the momentum as President Trump goes on the offensive against Russian President Vladimir Putin, though Republicans remain cautious about getting ahead of the commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Lithuanian president says Russian military planes violated the Baltic country's airspace
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Russian military planes briefly violated Lithuania’s airspace Thursday evening, the Lithuanian president said, condemning what he called a blatant breach of the territorial integrity of his European Union and NATO-member country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Krafton shifting to 'AI-first' game development
item.description: The makers of popular video game "PUBG: Battlegrounds" are switching gears and becoming an "AI-first" developer.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa lumbers through the Caribbean, killing an elderly man in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa lumbered through the Caribbean Sea on Thursday, bringing a risk of dangerous landslides and life-threatening flooding to Jamaica and southern Hispaniola — an island shared by the Dominican Republic and Haiti.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says declaration of war not needed for strikes on alleged cartel boats
item.description: President Trump on Thursday dismissed the need to ask Congress for a declaration of war as the U.S. military continuously launches strikes on boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean. “I don’t think we’re going to necessarily ask for a declaration of war,” Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: Albania’s Prince Leka engaged
item.description: The country’s crown prince has finally popped the question to his longtime girlfriend, photographer Blerta Celibashi.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of thousands of Hungarians attend rival rallies in Budapest as Orbán faces election test
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of Hungarians filled the streets of Budapest on Thursday in competing demonstrations as supporters of the country’s two main political movements staged mutual shows of strength before next spring’s national election.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US strikes two more alleged drug-carrying boats, this time in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military on Wednesday launched its ninth strike against an alleged drug-carrying vessel, killing three people in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said, expanding the Trump administration’s campaign against drug trafficking in South America.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PM Mark Carney says Canada will double its non-US exports
item.description: President Trump has been threatening Canada’s economy and sovereignty with tariffs, most offensively by claiming Canada could be “the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione brought up Brian Thompson's name while in Asia: Acquaintance
item.description: Christian Sacchini noted Luigi Mangione was shocked by how little an MRI scan cost in Thailand as opposed to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Louvre director acknowledges failure after jewel heist and says she offered to resign
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Louvre's director on Wednesday acknowledged a "terrible failure" at the Paris tourist attraction after a daylight crown jewel heist over the weekend, and said that she offered to resign but it was refused.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US carries out 8th strike on alleged drug vessel, this time in Eastern Pacific, Hegseth says
item.description: The U.S. military carried out another strike on a suspected drug vessel, this time in the Eastern Pacific, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dublin protesters clash with police, burn vehicle after migrant accused of sexually assaulting Irish girl
item.description: Dublin, Ireland, protests turned violent Tuesday and a police car was set ablaze after a migrant allegedly sexually assaulted a girl in state child care.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel 'not a protectorate' of the US, Netanyahu says ahead of meeting with Vance
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's prime minister toughened his stance Wednesday by declaring that his country is in charge of its own security and isn't an American protectorate as he prepared to discuss progress on Gaza’s fragile ceasefire agreement with U.S. Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How Louvre burglars obtained truck-mounted lift to make off with jewels worth more than $100M
item.description: Thieves used a stolen truck-mounted moving lift to scale the Louvre and steal royal jewels worth over $100 million in a lightning-fast Paris heist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles days before Trump's visit to the peninsula
item.description: North Korea test fired ballistic missiles days before President Donald Trump's Asia trip to meet China's Xi Jinping and other leaders in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massive Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 6, officials say
item.description: Ukraine’s energy minister said a “massive combined overnight attack” on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure was still underway early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel identifies 2 hostages returned from Gaza as residents of same kibbutz
item.description: Israel identified the two hostages returned from Gaza through the Red Cross as an 85-year-old man taken by Hamas and an IDF reserve master sergeant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paris prosecutor says stolen Louvre jewels worth an estimated $102 million
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Paris prosecutor said Tuesday that crown jewels stolen in a dramatic weekend Louvre heist were worth an estimated 88 million euros ($102 million), but that the monetary estimate doesn’t include their historical value to France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Vance says fragile Gaza ceasefire plan going better than expected
item.description: The U.S.-brokered deal has wavered amid renewed violence and uncertainty over next steps toward long-term peace.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia Giuffre, Epstein and Prince Andrew accuser, gets 'final word' in new memoir
item.description: The memoir serves as a discussion of surviving abuse, fighting for justice and suffering at the hands of Prince Andrew and disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Dad sends 11-year-old to bed thinking he was drunk; boy later dies of snake bite
item.description: The cause of death for an 11-year-old boy in Australia was confirmed last week by the Coroners Court of Queensland.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, NewsNation

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: China said any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Ukraine's Donbas region will have to be 'cut up' to end the Russian invasion
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said Sunday that the Donbas region of Ukraine should be “cut up," leaving most of it in Russian hands, to end a war that has dragged on for nearly four years. “Let it be cut the way it is,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
